markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games’ decision to delay Grand Theft Auto VI from May 26 to November 19, 2026, has extended the runway for preceding events and sharpened trader focus on cultural and industry milestones that could resolve in the interim. Official statements emphasize extra time for polish on the Vice City-set open-world title, aligning with Take-Two’s history of prioritizing quality over rushed launches. Recent earnings commentary and Rockstar updates have kept the November date as the current target, while broader gaming release calendars continue shifting around it. With roughly five months remaining, momentum centers on verifiable developments such as major music drops, tech announcements, or geopolitical shifts that traders view as likely to land before launch.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Harvey Weinstein’s legal outlook has shifted sharply following the May 2026 mistrial in his third New York rape case and continued appeals of the 16-year California conviction. At 74 and in declining health, Weinstein has already served more than six years; traders see these factors—combined with the possibility of concurrent sentencing or successful appeals citing prior evidentiary issues—driving the dominant 78.7% implied probability of no additional prison time. Recent plea discussions for unresolved charges and the lower-level nature of the remaining New York count further reinforce market sentiment that any new sentence is unlikely to extend incarceration materially. Upcoming appeal rulings and potential retrial developments remain the key near-term catalysts.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Final between the Vegas Golden Knights and Carolina Hurricanes features a closely contested best-of-seven series, with Vegas holding a 2-1 lead after dramatic early games marked by multiple comebacks, record-setting performances, and overtime thrillers. Vegas advanced by sweeping the Western Conference Final and has leveraged home-ice advantage at T-Mobile Arena alongside strong contributions from players like Mitch Marner, while Carolina demonstrated resilience in rallying from deficits to even the series temporarily. Trader consensus reflects these recent results and roster depth, positioning the Golden Knights as the slight favorite entering Game 4 amid ongoing playoff momentum for both Eastern and Western Conference champions.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 NBA Finals between the New York Knicks and San Antonio Spurs drive current trader consensus, with the Knicks holding a 2-1 series lead after dropping Game 3 on the road. New York finished the regular season at 53-29 as the East's third seed and has leaned on strong playoff form, including a franchise-best run to the championship round. The Spurs posted a league-best 62-20 mark as the West's second seed behind Victor Wembanyama's contributions but now trail after early losses at home. The best-of-seven format and upcoming games at Madison Square Garden keep the outcome open, though the Knicks' series position underpins their 62.3% implied probability versus the Spurs' 37.8%.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party, state institutions, and military through loyalist appointments and the absence of a designated successor, supporting trader expectations that he will remain paramount leader past 2027. Term limits on the presidency were eliminated in 2018, enabling a precedent-breaking third term in 2022, and analyses indicate he is positioned for a fourth term at the 2027 Party Congress. Recent diplomatic activity, including a May 2026 summit with U.S. President Trump and a planned June visit to North Korea, underscores his active role without signs of internal challenges or health-related constraints. Potential successors remain unpromoted to the Politburo Standing Committee, reinforcing continuity over transition.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom holds the largest share among a fragmented field of potential 2028 Democratic nominees, reflecting traders' emphasis on his California governorship and national visibility following the 2024 cycle. No formal announcements have occurred, leaving early positioning driven by name recognition, prior executive records, and outreach among governors and congressional figures. Key differentiators include governing experience for candidates like Newsom, Josh Shapiro, and Andy Beshear versus legislative or national profiles for Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Jon Ossoff, and Kamala Harris, alongside regional bases in the South and Midwest. The 2026 midterms and any subsequent polling shifts or endorsements could consolidate support, as the wide-open contest remains subject to rapid change before primary voters decide.
